bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no opponent history yet and no time pressure to concede. I therefore choose the top value on each issue, especially holding firm on issueC and issueD because they carry the highest importance in my preferences.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the very beginning of the negotiation, and the opponent’s only offer is far below my preferences. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid, I make a tiny concession on low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issueC and other high-value terms.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer to me without conceding. I will avoid repeating my exact previous bid, but I will only concede on the relatively low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issues, especially issueC and issueD.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.06), and the opponent has only made a small concession by moving issueD to my preferred valueC while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC. I will preserve my most important issueC and issueD positions, while making a small change on the lower-weight issueB to avoid repeating my previous bid and signal limited flexibility.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.08), but I am making a visible concession on low-weight issueA. The opponent has held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC while conceding issueD to my preferred valueC, so I will hold strongly on the high-value issueC and issueD while offering movement where it costs me less.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is little need to make major concessions on my most important issue, issueC. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA/issueB at valueA and briefly improved issueD to valueC, so I am conceding on the low-weight issueB while holding issueC and issueD at my preferred values.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by holding firm on my dominant issueC while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueB, which the opponent has repeatedly demanded as valueA. Since it is still early at t=0.12, I am not willing to concede issueC, but I can move issueD to valueD because the opponent’s last offer included it and it costs me only a small amount.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.74 because it is still early at t=0.14, so I should not concede on my most important issueC yet. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueA and issueB to valueA and has recently accepted/used issueD: valueC, so I concede the low-weight A/B issues while holding issueC at my top value and issueD at a strong mutually signaled value. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and their offers to me remain low overall.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain low for me, especially on the crucial issueC. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueA and issueB to valueA and has sometimes accepted issueD: valueC, so I concede on the low-weight issues while keeping issueD strong and moving issueC only slightly from valueC to valueD, which costs me little.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.72 while avoiding repeating my previous bid and still protecting the highest-weight issueC. The opponent has mostly held issueA/issueB at valueA and has recently offered issueC: valueC, so I concede on the lower-weight A/B issues and offer issueD: valueD as a middle ground they have previously proposed.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while conceding on the lower-weight issues A and B. The opponent has repeatedly offered valueA on issueA and issueB, but their offers are not steadily improving for me and the latest one regressed sharply, so I will hold firm on my most important issueC and strong issueD preference.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid (about 0.72 by my weights), conceding on the lower-weight issues A and B while holding firm on high-importance issueC. Time is still early at t=0.22, so I should not concede issueC; however, the opponent has repeatedly offered issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA and most recently issueD/valueD, so I can incorporate those signals while preserving my main value.

I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.74, preserving my highest-value issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC while conceding on the lower-weight issues A and B. Although t=0.24 is still early enough to hold firm, the opponent’s latest valid offer moved substantially toward my priorities by giving issueC and issueD my top values, so mirroring that offer is a good way to secure agreement without sacrificing my main interests.
